objective: Deliver a runnable local speech-output vertical slice that moderates the complete utterance before synthesis, normalizes and chunks Vietnamese text, lazily runs pinned VieNeu-TTS v3 Turbo CPU/ONNX inference with a licensed preset voice, returns binary WAV audio, exposes provider/job/alignment state, supports bounded admission and cancellation, and lets the Dev Console play or stop both manually-entered text and completed Hina chat replies.
non_goals: Do not clone the owner or any real person's voice in this slice.
Do not use MMS/F5-TTS or another non-commercial or unknown-license model as a fallback.
Do not generate canned, silent or placeholder audio when the real provider is unavailable.
Do not send text to the provider before the complete utterance passes the pre_tts moderation surface.
Do not claim the M05 naturalness, first-audio, RTF, reverse-ASR, barge-in or 1000-turn promotion gates from this fast-development slice.
Do not enable a CUDA TTS backend until it is protected by the shared ResourceLease scheduler.
contracts: The full normalized utterance must pass the existing pre_tts moderation surface before any provider model load or audio generation begins.
Blocked, quarantined, muted or emergency-stopped utterances produce zero audio bytes and a stable E_TTS_BLOCKED error with correlation ID.
The synthesis endpoint returns audio/wav bytes, never base64 audio in JSON, and never persists generated audio or input text.
Text is Unicode-normalized, bounded and split only at deterministic safe sentence boundaries; empty, control-bearing or oversized text fails closed.
VieNeu-TTS code is pinned to PyPI 3.2.3 and model/codec snapshots are downloaded only at recorded immutable revisions.
The default voice is the licensed built-in preset Trúc Ly. Voice cloning, reference audio and arbitrary voice files are unavailable.
The first runnable backend is CPU ONNX int8. CUDA is rejected until TTS ResourceLease integration exists.
Synthesis admission is bounded. Cancel marks the active utterance, stops browser playback immediately and asks the provider stream to stop at the next native chunk boundary.
Provider absence, model load failure, synthesis failure and cancellation return real state and never substitute fake audio.
Alignment events are estimated from emitted audio/chunk boundaries and labeled estimated; they are not claimed as phoneme-accurate visemes.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library and existing Hina runtime, safety and resource-scheduler code.
vieneu 3.2.3 from PyPI, pinned in uv.lock and used behind Hina's TtsProvider interface.
pnnbao-ump/VieNeu-TTS-v3-Turbo at revision 75ff82a72f54d55ed389e1eeb12041d3c4bac7d4 using its Apache-2.0 CPU ONNX int8 assets.
OpenMOSS-Team/MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-Nano-ONNX at revision ceff0d0749bfb3fa2d61149794ec6feef0d1e1ae using its Apache-2.0 codec assets.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover Unicode normalization, sentence chunking, text/body limits, complete-utterance moderation, zero-provider-call on block, WAV encoding, bounded admission, cancellation and no text/audio logging.
Provider tests prove model and codec snapshots are requested at exact revisions, CPU ONNX int8 is forced, only allowlisted preset voices are accepted and no reference audio/voice cloning path exists.
Runtime tests cover GET /v1/tts/status, binary POST /v1/tts/synthesis, POST /v1/tts/utterances/{id}/cancel, correlation headers and stable failures.
Dev Console can synthesize owner-entered text, play the real WAV, stop playback, show provider/voice/timing state and optionally auto-speak a completed moderated Hina chat response.
Starting microphone capture stops current playback and requests cancellation so user barge-in does not leave local audio playing.

A real VieNeu CPU smoke generates a non-empty valid WAV using the pinned Trúc Ly preset; the runtime retains nothing, while the explicit owner smoke tool may leave its ignored var/tmp WAV for manual listening.
Fast unit, contract, governance/provenance and Dev Console startup smoke remain green after integration.
required_test_commands: uv run --frozen python -m unittest discover -s tests/governance -p "test_*.py"
